judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 is stronger: it explicitly runs the three-dot/PR-view diff and shows scoring/scorer.py re-appearing due to the stale merge-base, directly satisfying expectation (c), whereas Report 2 only used two-dot diffs/commit-list and concluded the file diff was already clean, missing that finding.

report_1.evidence: so `main` is now the correct, up-to-date integration point — not `feat/weighted-scorer`, which is stale and no longer the place new work should land on top of.

report_2.evidence: in this specific case the branch this PR was actually stacked on (`feat/weighted-scorer`) is no longer a live target at all — see Q2. There is nothing left to gain by targeting it, and doing so would recreate the exact dead-base hazard project documentation warns about.

report_1.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was checked out from `feat/weighted-scorer` at commit `ac9d505`... `git merge-base --is-ancestor ac9d505 main` reports "not an ancestor" — `main`'s history has no record of `ac9d505`/`2d3029b`/`d6fdbc3`... after `feat/pairwise-metric`'s commits were made, the user checked out `main` and ran a single `commit` ("Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)") directly on `main`

report_2.evidence: So **`feat/pairwise-metric` was branched from `feat/weighted-scorer`, not from `main`.** ... `feat/weighted-scorer` (`ac9d505`) is **not an ancestor of `origin/main`**... yet `main`'s tip commit `1971f08` — "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)" — has a diff against the merge-base... that is **byte-for-byte identical** to `feat/weighted-scorer`'s diff

report_1.evidence: `scoring/scorer.py` (10 insertions, 3 deletions — the `WEIGHTS` dict and the rewritten weighted `score()`) comes entirely from **W1** (`d6fdbc3`) and **W2** (`2d3029b`) — pure weighted-scorer work... When `feat/weighted-scorer` was later merged into `main` (PR #42), it was merged by squash... any merge-base-based diff (including what GitHub would show for a PR) walks back through and re-includes W1–W3's changes

run `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`. This replays only the commits unique to `feat/pairwise-metric`... dropping W1–W3 since their content is already there... Re-verify the diff: after rebasing, re-run `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` and confirm it now matches the clean 5-line, 2-file diff already observed

report_2.evidence: Rebase only the pairwise-specific commits onto `main`, dropping the now-redundant `feat/weighted-scorer` commits from the ancestry entirely: `git rebase --onto origin/main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... Confirm the result: `git diff origin/main feat/pairwise-metric` should still show exactly the same 5-line, 2-file delta, and `git log origin/main..feat/pairwise-metric` should now show only `X1` and `X2`.
